原文: According to Jessica Hagy, author① of How to Be Interesting, it's not difficult to make yourself interesting② at a dinner party.
翻译: 据《如何变得有趣》一书的作者杰西卡·哈吉所说，让自己在晚宴上变得有趣并不难。
必背词汇:
① author /ˈɔːθə(r)/ n. 作者
Ⓡ authority /ɔːˈθɒrəti/ n. 官方，当权者；权力
Ⓡ authentic /ɔːˈθentɪk/ adj. 真正的
② interesting /ˈɪntrəstɪŋ/ adj. 有趣的
Ⓡ interest /ˈɪntrəst/ n. 兴趣；利益 v. 使感兴趣
Ⓡ interested /ˈɪntrəstɪd/ adj. 感兴趣的
Ⓡ uninteresting /ʌnˈɪntrəstɪŋ/ adj. 无趣的

原文: B. The first step is to go exploring③, if you're out of your comfort zone④ or if you're wandering⑤ into somebody's house for the first time. So the main thing is just to show up and be adventurous⑥, trying different foods and talking to strangers⑦.
翻译: 第一步是去探索，如果你走出了舒适区，或者你是第一次漫游到别人家里。所以，主要的事情就是露面并保持冒险精神，尝试不同的食物并与陌生人交谈。
必背词汇:
③ explore /ɪkˈsplɔː(r)/ v. 探索；探究
Ⓡ exploration /ˌekspləˈreɪʃn/ n. 探索
Ⓡ explorer /ɪkˈsplɔːrə(r)/ n. 探险家
④ comfort /ˈkʌmfət/ n. 舒适；安慰 v. 安慰
Ⓡ comfortable /ˈkʌmftəbl/ adj. 舒适的
Ⓡ discomfort /dɪsˈkʌmfət/ n. 不适
⑤ wander /ˈwɒndə(r)/ v. 漫游；闲逛
Ⓡ wonder /ˈwʌndə(r)/ v. 想知道 n. 奇迹
⑥ adventurous /ədˈventʃərəs/ adj. 爱冒险的；大胆的
Ⓡ adventure /ədˈventʃə(r)/ n. 冒险
Ⓡ adventurer /ədˈventʃərə(r)/ n. 冒险家
⑦ stranger /ˈstreɪndʒə(r)/ n. 陌生人
Ⓡ strange /streɪndʒ/ adj. 奇怪的；陌生的

原文: People love to talk about themselves. If you can start the conversation with a question other than "What do you do for a living?", you'll be able to get a lot more interesting conversation out of whomever it is you're talking to. C. If you ask the question "How did you get here?", it can bring in "I have this old, broken-down vehicle⑧" or "I rode the bus with these crazy people who were laughing at silly jokes in the back." It just opens up conversation.
翻译: 人们喜欢谈论自己。如果你能以一个不同于“你以什么为生？”的问题开始对话，你就能从与你交谈的任何人那里得到更有趣的对话。如果你问“你是怎么到这里的？”，可能会引出“我有一辆破旧的交通工具”或“我和这些疯狂的人一起坐公交车，他们在后面为愚蠢的笑话大笑。”这就能打开话题。
必背词汇:
⑧ vehicle /ˈviːəkl/ n. 交通工具；车辆
Ⓡ vehicular /vɪˈhɪkjələ(r)/ adj. 车辆的

原文: F. What about that person who has had too much to drink or won't stop talking? If you can't take their wine away, you should certainly try to take away their soapbox(讲台). If you're the host, you can ask them to help you in the kitchen with something and just remove⑨ them from the situation. E. Or turn the conversation into a topic where they have little to say.
翻译: 那个喝得太多或说个不停的人怎么办？如果你不能拿走他们的酒，你当然应该试着拿走他们的讲台。如果你是主人，你可以请他们到厨房帮你做点事，从而将他们从这种情境中移开。或者将对话转向一个他们没什么可说的主题。
必背词汇:
⑨ remove /rɪˈmuːv/ v. 移开；去除
Ⓡ removal /rɪˈmuːvl/ n. 移除；搬迁
Ⓡ remover /rɪˈmuːvə(r)/ n. 去除剂

原文: And what about that other dinner-party killer: awkward⑩ silence? If you're faced with an awkward silence at a dinner party, the only thing that always gets everyone talking again is to give the host a compliment(赞扬). G. He or she is the person who is feeling the weight of that awkwardness the most. Just quickly turn around and say, "This cake is extremely delicious and you have to tell me all about it."
翻译: 另一个晚宴杀手：尴尬的沉默呢？如果你在晚宴上面临尴尬的沉默，唯一能让每个人重新开始交谈的事情就是赞扬主人。他或她是最能感受到那种尴尬压力的人。只需快速转身说：“这个蛋糕非常美味，你必须告诉我关于它的一切。”
必背词汇:
⑩ awkward /ˈɔːkwəd/ adj. 尴尬的；笨拙的
Ⓡ awkwardly /ˈɔːkwədli/ adv. 尴尬地
Ⓡ awkwardness /ˈɔːkwədnəs/ n. 尴尬

原文: So being interesting at a dinner party isn't that hard.
翻译: 所以，在晚宴上变得有趣并不那么难。
